main_event: Civilian in Syracuse observed star-like object alternating red and green on 30 November 1967, concluded probably Betelgeuse.
sighted_object: str like object that alternated between red and green
witness_description: civilian and wife observing stars through telescope at 809 Wolf Street Syracuse
interesting_points: Object in the east at elevation of about 45 deg, alternating between red and green
Observer and wife were observing stars through a telescope
Observer later told investigator he believes he and his wife were observing planet Saturn
Saturn was roughly in SW at elevation 40 degs at time of sighting
Betelgeuse was at azimuth 110 degs and elevation 41 degs
No unusual radar incidents reported at Direction Center
First of three reports on this one object
